name: evo2_phage_genome_fragment
description: Sample 1024 bp phage-like DNA fragments from Evo2 (the 7B genome-scale autoregressive DNA model; Nguyen et al. 2024) seeded by a canonical bacterial gene-start prompt, then keep the best draws by three biological filters: (1) `mmseqs-gene-similarity` against the PHROGs phage protein database (Terzian et al. 2021) with the percent-identity range 30-80%, so the design is recognizably phage-like but not a direct copy of a known protein; (2) `gc-content` 40-55%, the typical range for tailed-phage genomes (Almpanis et al. 2018); (3) `max-homopolymer` 5, a synthesis-friendly cap that also avoids the long mononucleotide runs that confuse Prodigal/orfipy ORF callers. Rejection-sampling is the right optimizer for a fully autoregressive generator: every sample is a fresh draw from the Evo2 prior conditioned on the prompt, so the optimizer's job is simply to score and rank, not to perturb a current state. For local refinement of a single Evo2 draft via random-nucleotide mutations, layer an MCMC stage on top using the `random-nucleotide` generator (cf. `protein_quality_baseline.json` for the protein-side analogue).
